content: Targeted therapy blocks specific molecules driving cancer growth. Oncogene drivers are mutated or overexpressed proteins that promote tumorigenesis. Tyrosine kinase inhibitors (TKIs) block intracellular signaling: EGFR inhibitors (erlotinib, osimertinib) for lung cancer; ALK inhibitors (crizotinib, alectinib) for ALK-rearranged NSCLC; BRAF inhibitors (vemurafenib, dabrafenib) for BRAF V600E melanoma; HER2 inhibitors (lapatinib, tucatinib) for breast cancer. Monoclonal antibodies target cell surface receptors: trastuzumab (HER2), cetuximab (EGFR), bevacizumab (VEGF). Other targets: PARP inhibitors (olaparib) for BRCA-mutated cancers; CDK4/6 inhibitors (palbociclib) for hormone receptor-positive breast cancer; mTOR inhibitors (everolimus); BTK inhibitors (ibrutinib) for CLL. Precision medicine matches drugs to molecular alterations regardless of tumor origin.
